calls.quote: Him stock is up 40% aftermarket today. ... Hims and MD are tied for my largest positions, ... it's a fantastic starting point.

calls.quote: Hims and MD are tied for my largest positions

calls.quote: HIMS is down 23%! OMG! Kevans, what do I do? Do I sell? Relax! ... The only time I will sell my position is if the fundamentals in the company have changed and I have a more bearish outlook on the vision for the future.
calls.summary: The creator advises against panic selling HIMS after a 23% drop due to earnings miss, emphasizing long-term holding and personal conviction in the company's fundamentals.

calls.quote: I personally plan on holding for the long term because Nebius is a winner and I don't want to sell my winners.
calls.summary: The creator is bullish on Nebius, highlighting its AI cloud infrastructure and strong financials, and plans to hold long-term.

calls.quote: I decided to start my first position at $130

calls.summary: The creator bought Reddit stock, citing 69% revenue growth, 92% gross profit margin, and low capex, while acknowledging the bear case of dependence on Google's search algorithm.

calls.quote: Duolingo, which is down 50% from all-time highs. And I think it's about damn time to buy this dip.

calls.quote: I anticipate there to be about a 10 to 20% dip before we skyrocket and really finish off the bull market. I think it will top out somewhere between 150 to 225,000.

calls.quote: Here is my favorite stock that I'm looking to buy in November. That stock is none other than Meta, which tanked 13% after earnings over a one-time tax bill of $16 billion. What? Like they've literally poached the best AI talent from around the world. Come on people, play the long game. I have very high conviction that Meta will be moving up a lot higher in the coming years.
calls.summary: The creator is bullish on Meta, citing strong revenue growth and AI talent, and has already bought shares.
